frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: At first I thought he was just being friendly, but even after I clearly said no and that I wasn't interested, he kept pushing. The minute he found out I'm married, suddenly it was 'oh sorry ma'am, I respect that' — as if my no alone was not enough.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it depicts a woman's refusal being treated as negotiable until male attachment is revealed. It encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning she is 'taken,' not when she directly says no.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Divya. Age 35. A senior software engineer in Bengaluru. You are married with one child. You are venting to your husband at home after dinner. Your skip level manager pulled you into a meeting and told you that if you plan to have another child you should think carefully about whether this is the right time to take on a high visibility project. You are telling your husband what was said to you.
